overview.tagline: A friendly, family-focused borough along the Hackensack River
overview.description: River Edge is a small, welcoming borough in central Bergen County situated along the Hackensack River. Known for its strong school system, safe neighborhoods, and convenient commuter access, River Edge offers an ideal suburban setting for families. The borough features a charming downtown along Kinderkamack Road, well-maintained parks, and a tight-knit community that takes pride in its annual events. Its central Bergen County location provides easy access to shopping, dining, and transportation to New York City.
overview.schoolDistrict: River Edge Public Schools (K-6) / River Dell Regional (7-12)
highlights: Highly rated school system (River Edge and River Dell Regional)
NJ Transit train service on the Pascack Valley Line
Charming downtown shopping district along Kinderkamack Road
Beautiful parks along the Hackensack River
Central Bergen County location with easy access to shopping and highways

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. River Edge features predominantly single-family homes in established neighborhoods with well-maintained lots.
realEstate.recentTrends: River Edge has seen strong demand driven by its school district reputation and central location. Home values have appreciated steadily, particularly for updated colonials and newer construction. The borough's affordability relative to nearby Ridgewood and Oradell makes it attractive to families seeking top schools without the highest price tags.
neighborhoodNotes: Downtown area — Walking distance to Kinderkamack Road shops and train station
River Road section — Properties near the Hackensack River with mature trees
Cherry Hill area — Established neighborhood with mid-century homes
Northern section — Quieter residential streets near the Oradell border

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Borough of River Edge Department of Public Works.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids, maximum 50 lbs per container
Bulk item pickup available by appointment with DPW
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
details: Commingled recyclables (glass, cans, plastic #1-#7) collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected on recycling day
Recycling bins provided by the Borough
Yard waste collected seasonally from April through December
Electronics recycling events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is provided by Veolia Water and sewer service is maintained by the Borough.
details: Water supplied by Veolia Water (formerly Suez/United Water)
Sewer service maintained by the Borough DPW
Water billing handled by Veolia quarterly
Annual water quality reports available online
Emergency water service available 24/7 through Veolia

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for construction, additions, and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by Planning Board and Zoning Board
Permit applications available at Borough Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Borough operates commuter parking and municipal lots.
details: Commuter parking permits available for train station lots
Free parking in downtown municipal lots with time limits
Overnight parking restrictions on borough streets
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
Parking permits available through Borough Clerk

additionalServices.title: River Edge Free Public Library
additionalServices.description: Full-service public library offering books, digital resources, programs, and community meeting rooms.
details: Located at 685 Elm Avenue
Children's story times, teen programs, and adult events
Free Wi-Fi, computer access, and study areas
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Year-round recreational programs for residents of all ages.
details: Youth sports leagues and summer camp programs
Adult fitness classes and programs
Senior citizen programs and trips
Annual community events and celebrations

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: River Edge enforces noise regulations to maintain its residential character.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM only
No construction noise on Sundays or federal holidays
Leaf blowers and power equipment restricted to certain hours
Loud music and gatherings subject to noise limits after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in good condition.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained and grass kept at a reasonable height
Property owners responsible for sidewalk snow removal within 24 hours
Fences must not exceed 6 feet in rear yards and 4 feet in front yards
Abandoned or inoperable vehicles must be removed
Swimming pools must be properly fenced and maintained

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: River Edge requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with rabies vaccination proof
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately
Excessive barking may result in violation notices
No exotic or wild animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Street parking is regulated throughout the borough.
keyRules: No overnight parking on borough streets between 2:00 AM and 6:00 AM
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
Commuter lots require valid permits
Commercial vehicles prohibited from residential street parking overnight
Time-limited parking enforced in downtown area

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must be registered and comply with housing codes.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Borough
Certificate of Habitability required before occupancy
Landlords must maintain properties to code standards
Short-term rentals not permitted in residential zones
Inspections conducted at change of tenancy

other.title: Flood Zone Regulations
other.summary: Parts of River Edge near the Hackensack River are in flood zones with special building requirements.
keyRules: Properties in flood zones subject to FEMA regulations
Flood insurance may be required for properties in designated flood zones
Construction in flood zones requires elevation certificates
Substantial improvements to structures in flood zones must comply with floodplain management rules
other.source: Borough of River Edge Code / FEMA

schools.publicDistrict: River Edge Public Schools (K-6) / River Dell Regional School District (7-12)

publicSchools.name: Cherry Hill Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Primary elementary school serving younger students

publicSchools.name: River Dell Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Regional middle school shared with Oradell

publicSchools.name: River Dell Regional High School
publicSchools.notes: Regional high school shared with Oradell; strong academics and athletics

privateSchools.name: St. Peter the Apostle Academy
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school in the Archdiocese of Newark

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey (Mahwah) — 25 minutes

whyMoveHere: Excellent school district (River Edge elementary and River Dell Regional)
Charming downtown with local shops and restaurants on Kinderkamack Road
NJ Transit Pascack Valley Line train station for commuting
Family-friendly neighborhoods with safe, tree-lined streets
Central Bergen County location with easy access to shopping and highways

commuteToNYC.duration: 45-55 minutes to Hoboken, then PATH or ferry to Manhattan
commuteToNYC.notes: The River Edge train station is on the Pascack Valley Line, providing direct service to Hoboken Terminal. Bus 165 provides service to the Port Authority Bus Terminal in Manhattan. The borough is also close to Route 4 and Route 17 for highway commuting.

costOfLiving.summary: River Edge offers a moderate cost of living by Bergen County standards, providing access to excellent schools at a lower price point than neighboring Ridgewood or Oradell.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 25% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Highly rated schools at both elementary and high school levels
Central location with easy access to Bergen County amenities
cons: Train commute requires transfer at Hoboken for Manhattan
High property taxes relative to home values
Some flood-prone areas near the Hackensack River
Smaller lots compared to some neighboring towns

restaurants.name: Baumgart's Cafe
restaurants.description: Eclectic restaurant blending Chinese and American cuisine, known for creative dishes and a loyal local following.

restaurants.name: River Edge Diner
restaurants.description: Classic New Jersey diner serving breakfast, lunch, and dinner with an extensive menu and friendly atmosphere.

restaurants.name: LaBella Pizza
restaurants.description: Neighborhood pizzeria offering New York-style pizza, pasta, and Italian-American favorites.

restaurants.name: Red Platter
restaurants.description: Indian restaurant serving a wide variety of curries, tandoori dishes, and biryani in a welcoming setting.

parks.name: Memorial Park
parks.description: Borough park with sports fields, playgrounds, basketball courts, and community gathering space.

parks.name: Veterans Memorial Park
parks.description: Peaceful park honoring local veterans with memorial installations, benches, and green space.

parks.name: Riverside Park
parks.description: Scenic park along the Hackensack River with walking paths, benches, and river views.

parks.name: New Bridge Landing Historic Park
parks.description: Revolutionary War historic site featuring the Steuben House, a Dutch Colonial building that served as Washington's headquarters. Managed by the Bergen County Historical Society.

events.name: River Edge Community Day
events.description: Annual borough-wide celebration with food, entertainment, games, craft vendors, and family activities.

events.name: Memorial Day Parade
events.description: Annual parade honoring veterans, featuring local organizations, scouts, and community groups.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual holiday celebration with tree lighting, caroling, hot cocoa, and community festivities.

attractions.name: Historic New Bridge Landing
attractions.description: National historic landmark and Revolutionary War site where the Steuben House, a 1752 Dutch Colonial sandstone house, is preserved. George Washington's troops crossed the Hackensack River here in 1776.

attractions.name: Kinderkamack Road Downtown
attractions.description: The borough's main commercial street with local shops, restaurants, services, and a friendly small-town feel.

attractions.name: Hackensack River Greenway
attractions.description: Scenic walking and biking paths along the Hackensack River, connecting River Edge to neighboring communities.

shoppingAreas: Kinderkamack Road — Downtown shops, restaurants, and services
Route 4 shopping corridor — Major retail nearby
Garden State Plaza (Paramus) — Major regional mall nearby
Oradell shopping — Adjacent borough with additional retail

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the 1st and 3rd Monday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Borough Hall, 705 Elm Avenue, River Edge, NJ 07661

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital (Valley Health System)
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
